name: Visual Novel: Requiem for Noodles
short_description: After being hit on the head with a stool, Dimon goes straight to Hell to find out where their now deceased buddy Seryoga hid the last pack of instant noodles.
detailed_description:
Meet Dimon. In his dreams, he's an unrecognized philosopher, the terror of the neighborhood, and the frontman of an underground metal band. In reality, he's a skinny guy in a washed-out hoodie, three days into a starvation diet, desperately trying to remember where his late buddy Seryoga hid his last pack of chicken-flavored noodles.

The verdict of his starving friends was harsh but fair: "Time to straighten out some sense." A brief encounter with a flying stool sends Dimon into a deep stupor, right on the other side of reality. Destination: Hell.

Everything would be fine: warmth from free boilers, no utilities, and eternal peace. But there's one problem with the taste of chicken broth. It was here, in the bureaucratic depths of Hell, that treasured pack somehow mysteriously disappeared.

You have less than 24 hours to find the "artifact," outsmart the Demon-Watchman, survive the endless line to see the Receptionist, and return to your body before your friends bury you under the lilac tree outside your building.

KEY FEATURES:

• The CIS version of Hell we deserve: Forget about rivers of sulfur. The real underworld is made up of clinics with perpetual lunches, thugs squatting, and elevators scarier than a cauldron full of sinners.

• Entrance Logic: To solve the puzzles here, you'll have to think not like a normal person, but like Dimon. Absurdity, bureaucracy, and a pinch of arrogance are your main weapons.

• Time is ticking: The clock is ticking! Your physical body is freezing, and the boys are already looking for a shovel. A race against time atmosphere, where sacred Doshirak and your own life are at stake. • Unique characters: The local inhabitants are more frightening than Satan. Outsmart the demon-dealer Ashot at shell game, negotiate with the three-headed Cerberus, and find a way to approach Baba Nyura, the final boss of this panel purgatory.

• No choice: Every decision you make leads either to the coveted noodles or to death by laughter, a stool, or the embrace of the muscle-bound succubi.

You must navigate all seven circles of bureaucratic Hell to prove: home is where they're waiting for you with boiling water and a stool.

reviews.review_text:
[h1]🍜 From Russia With Noodles: A Hellish Comedy Worth Tasting 🎮[/h1]

🎲 What if the gates of Hell opened not for your sins, but for a packet of instant noodles? [b]Visual Novel: Requiem for Noodles[/b] tells the story of Dimon (Dmitry), who gets knocked unconscious with a stool to the head and wakes up in Hell. His mission: find his dead buddy Seroga (Sergey) and the last packet of chicken-flavored noodles before his friends bury him under the lilac bush by their apartment block. This March 2026 release from [b]Reanim Games[/b] and publisher [b]STuNT[/b] delivers something genuinely unique for those who understand the culture.

📖 The premise is beautifully absurd. [b]CIS-style Hell[/b] replaces fire and brimstone with polyclinics, bureaucratic queues, and elevator rides scarier than any demon pit. The black humor is rooted in post-Soviet reality, particularly the [u]1990s Perestroika era[/u]. Anyone who lived through that time in USSR or CIS countries will recognize the cultural references immediately. Some players now living abroad but originally from CIS regions will also connect with these jokes. For those unfamiliar with this cultural context, many references may require explanation.

🎨 All artwork is [i]generated by artificial intelligence[/i], which creates a distinctive visual style. The colorful presentation matches the absurd tone perfectly. Character designs include memorable demons like [b]Ashot the card shark[/b], [b]three-headed intelligent Cerberus[/b], and [b]Baba Nyura as final boss[/b]. These characters embody the post-Soviet archetypes that make the humor work. Note that [u]Russian text appears on some images[/u] (walls, elevators, signs) which remains untranslated.

⚖️ This is a [b]classic visual novel[/b] - you read text, view images, and click to progress. There are [u]no RPG mechanics[/u], [u]no time pressure[/u], and [u]no dungeon exploration[/u] in the traditional sense. The "seven circles of Hell" are narrative chapters, not gameplay areas. You can read at your own pace, scroll back to re-read, or rush through in minutes - though that defeats the purpose. The [u]multiple endings[/u] come from story choices, not stats or skills.

[h2]✅ What Makes This Special[/h2]
[list]
[*]✅ Brilliantly absurd premise that commits fully to its concept.
[*]✅ Authentic CIS cultural humor that feels genuine and specific.
[*]✅ Memorable cast of demonic characters with distinct post-Soviet personalities.
[*]✅ Relaxed pacing - read and enjoy at your own speed.
[*]✅ Multiple endings encourage replay to discover all story branches.
[*]✅ Seven circles structure provides satisfying narrative progression.
[*]✅ Serves as cultural snapshot of 1990s post-Soviet life.
[/list]

[h2]❌ Important Considerations[/h2]
[list]
[*]❌ AI-generated art may not appeal to all visual novel fans.
[*]❌ CIS-specific humor requires cultural context to fully appreciate.
[*]❌ Only 2 languages available at launch.
[*]❌ Russian text on in-game images remains untranslated.
[*]❌ Younger players or those unfamiliar with 1990s USSR life may miss many references.
[*]❌ Dark themes include death, smoking, and hell imagery.
[*]❌ Purely narrative experience - no traditional gameplay mechanics.
[/list]

[h2]🎯 Final Verdict[/h2]

🏆 [b]Visual Novel: Requiem for Noodles[/b] is a [u]culturally specific comedy[/u] that succeeds for its target audience. The combination of post-Soviet absurdism with supernatural comedy creates something fresh for those who understand the references. Dimon's journey through bureaucratic Hell for a packet of noodles is ridiculous enough to work brilliantly.

📊 For players who [b]lived through the 1990s in USSR or CIS countries[/b], this deserves attention. The humor, characters, and situations will feel familiar and hilarious. This also appeals to [b]cultural researchers[/b] and those interested in post-Soviet life - some regions still resemble what is depicted here. For others, this may feel like an interesting but distant cultural artifact.

🍜 Here is a pro tip: playing this while eating a packet of instant noodles yourself makes the experience [i]deliciously meta[/i]. That cheap bowl of noodles will taste infinitely richer when you understand its [b]hellish value[/b] in this story.

💭 For anyone currently living similarly to the heroes - surviving on cheap instant noodles - this game might serve as [u]healthy self-irony[/u]. Sometimes laughing at fictional characters struggling for the same meal helps you realize your own situation is not quite as catastrophic. A small smile can make tough times feel lighter.

🔗 [b]Recommended[/b] for fans of quirky visual novels, black comedy enthusiasts, CIS culture natives, and anyone curious about 1990s post-Soviet life. [b]Skip This One[/b] if you need traditional gameplay mechanics, require full localization, or lack the cultural context for the jokes.

[url=https://gamefaqs.gamespot.com/pc/628302-visual-novel-requiem-for-noodles/data]Check GameFAQs for additional release details[/url]

🎭 Sometimes the best stories come from the most ridiculous premises - and this bowl of noodles proves it perfectly for those who understand the recipe.
1000-7 or this game ? The choice is obvious!
A real base, not a game! Just like in real life!
